title: Ecbolium longiflorum
taxonomy: Ecbolium longiflorum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Ecbolium, which comprises several species of plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is further classified under the phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Lamiales. Ecbolium longiflorum is a relatively rare species, and its classification is mainly based on its morphology and taxonomic characteristics. Further research may be required to confirm its exact taxonomic position within the Acanthaceae family.

intro: Ecbolium longiflorum, also known as the Long-flowered Ecbolium, is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae, characterized by its unique, long-flowered spikes and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ecbolium longiflorum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ecbolium longiflorum is commonly used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory infections, fever, and other health conditions.
